reasoning: The question asks if EQR's price on March 22, 2026 (weekend, so March 20 Friday close) will be higher than March 15, 2026 (weekend, so March 13 Friday close, which was $59.27). In general, 1-week stock movements are largely unpredictable and approximate a random walk with a slight upward drift (around 52% baseline). The YFinance tool’s statistical trend analysis indicates a linear model estimate of P(increase) = 0.55, and a combined estimate of 0.61. The recent momentum has been downward, with the stock dropping from ~$62.35 down to $59.27 over the preceding week. The likely ex-dividend date for Q1 2026 will not fall within the March 13–March 20 window (as last year's was late March / record date Mar 31), averting any mechanical price drops that could assure a "No" resolution. Given the lack of specific negative catalysts in this window and the baseline expectation coupled with mild mean reversion (as indicated by the trend tool), the probability of the stock closing higher on March 20 than on March 13 is somewhat better than a coin flip, but bounded by the inherent unpredictability of short-term stock prices. Therefore, an estimate of 0.56 balances the statistical anchor and the noisy reality of weekly asset pricing.
